title: Promote drift check into make automation-check
summary: Folded `python3 Tools/localization-review-drift-check.py --check` into the `make automation-check` recipe so any drift between source strings and per-locale review return files now fails the gate (exit non-zero). The standalone `make localization-review-drift-check` target remains reporting-only for ad-hoc inspection. Order in the gate: pyright → drift-check --check → automation Python tests; the first failure short-circuits subsequent steps.
files_changed: Makefile (automation-check recipe gains drift-check --check before unittest discover)
docs/workflows/validation.md (automation-check description + standalone drift-check bullet updated)

make automation-check sequence: 2. python3 Tools/localization-review-drift-check.py --check (new — exits non-zero on any drift)
gate_semantics.reporting_only_target_preserved: make localization-review-drift-check (no --check) still works for ad-hoc inspection

not_claimed: make automation-check now also runs make ui-regression (it doesn't; ui-regression remains a separate gate)
the drift gate covers stringsdict format-specifier integrity (intentionally not tracked; metadata changes don't count as translation drift)
make localization-review-drift-check (reporting-only) is the gate (it isn't; only --check inside automation-check fails on drift)
residual_risk: If a future source-string change creates drift, every agent or CI invocation of make automation-check will fail until either (a) the affected return file entries are refreshed (separate review slice) or (b) the source change is reverted. The fail-loud behavior is the intended gate semantics; the residual risk is just that it now imposes a cost on contributors who add new English copy without a paired native-review intake. Document the expectation in localization-translation-quality.md if the cost becomes a friction point.

The current baseline 0 drift was preserved across the full localization HIG closure. If a contributor's branch sits on stale return files (e.g., they merged in source-string changes from main but the review intake is behind), they would see this gate fail on their branch but not on main.
